title: Space-Saving Wall Bike Rack
summary: A simple 2x4 wall frame with hooks that hangs bikes vertically off the floor — build the frame flat, add the hooks, then hang it into wall studs.
description:
This rack is deliberately minimal: a rectangular 2x4 frame that hangs flat against a wall, with hooks screwed to its face at spacing sized for standard bikes. The whole appeal is floor space — hanging bikes by their front wheel or top tube clears a garage or mudroom floor that flat bike storage never does.

Build the frame on the ground first, square, with two screws per corner joint. Space the hooks before hanging anything — the plan's spacing is sized for standard adult bikes, but measure your own bikes' handlebar width and tire size if they're unusual, and adjust before you commit to hook placement.

**This rack must go into real wall studs, not drywall anchors alone.** A loaded bike rack is a genuine pull-out load on whatever it's screwed to, and the plan calls for a minimum of four screws into studs. Hang the frame so the bottom row of bikes clears the floor by a few inches — that clearance is the entire point of building this over just leaning bikes against the wall.

materials.name: Bike hooks or large J hooks, surface mount

steps.title: Build the frame
steps.body: Build the rectangular frame on the ground from the frame ends and sides, using two 3" screws per corner joint.

steps.title: Add the hooks
steps.body: Space and attach the bike hooks to the face of the frame. The spacing works for standard bikes, but measure your own bikes and adjust if they're unusually wide or have oversized tires.
materials: Bike hooks or large J hooks, surface mount

steps.title: Hang the rack
steps.body: Locate wall studs and hang the frame with a minimum of four screws into studs, positioned so the bottom row of bikes clears the floor. Check level before final tightening.

unresolvedImages.alt: Wall-mounted 2x4 frame bike rack with hooks
